channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: U of M says it's committed to combating antisemitism amid federal probes
item.description: The University of Minnesota says it’s working to address antisemitism on campus, amid federal investigations spurred by protests against Israel. Recent federal actions are raising alarms for pro-Palestinian and civil rights advocates, who say it’s a violation of free speech.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: US judge temporarily halts Trump plan to cut hundreds of millions of dollars for teacher training
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge in Boston on Tuesday blocked the Trump administration's plan to cut hundreds of millions of dollars for teacher training, finding that cuts are already affecting training programs aimed at addressing a nationwide teacher shortage. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lone Democrat to back House GOP spending bill explains decision
item.description: Rep. Jared Golden (D-Maine) defied House Democratic leadership on Tuesday when he voted for the GOP’s bill to avert a government shutdown — the only one in the caucus to support the stopgap. Golden’s “yes” vote, to be sure, was not decisive, since Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Virginia’s Earle-Sears drops first ad confronting Dem opponent over migrant crime, border wall opposition
item.description: An ad from Virginia Lt. Gov. Winsome Earle-Sears plays clips of former Rep. Abigail Spanberger's comments, including her opposition to Trump's border wall, and news clippings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Admitted Hezbollah terrorist to be deported after entering US illegally under Biden's watch
item.description: An illegal immigrant who admitted to being linked to a terrorist network is slated to be deported, the New York Post reported on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blue state suing sheriff’s office for helping enforce immigration law
item.description: Washington AG Nick Brown is suing a sheriff’s office in his state for “illegally collaborating" with federal officials and the Trump administration to enforce immigration law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Buying Time
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Law firm sues over Trump executive order that seeks to suspend security clearances
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A law firm targeted by President Donald Trump over its legal services during the 2016 presidential campaign sued the federal government Tuesday over an executive order that seeks to strip its attorneys of security clearances.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Court asked to intervene after email tells USAID workers to destroy classified documents
item.description: A union for U.S. Agency for International Development contractors is asking a federal judge to intervene in any destruction of USAID classified documents.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer And Farnoush Amiri, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Education Department cuts half its staff as Trump vows to wind the agency down
item.description: The Education Department plans to lay off more than 1,300 of its employees as part of an effort to halve the organization’s staff -- a prelude to President Donald Trump’s plan to dismantle the agency.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: House Republicans pass bill to avert government shutdown
item.description: The House on Tuesday passed a funding bill to avert an end-of-the-week government shutdown, teeing up the measure for consideration in the Senate. The chamber cleared the continuing resolution (CR) in a largely party-line 217-213 vote, with just one Democrat — Rep.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump selects a new Tesla on White House driveway to show support for Elon Musk
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump shopped for a new Tesla on the White House driveway on Tuesday, selecting a shiny red sedan to show his support for Elon Musk 's electric vehicle company as it faces blowback because of his work to advance the president's political agenda and downsize the fed
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kentucky bourbon makers fear becoming 'collateral damage' in Trump's trade war
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — With a new distillery set to open soon, the makers of Brough Brothers bourbon in Kentucky were ready to put their business plan into action. They were looking to ramp up whiskey production to break into lucrative new markets in Canada and Europe.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US-Canada products are 99% tariff-free: Canadian ambassador to US
item.description: Canada's ambassador to the United States said 99% of products traded between the two nations are tariff-free.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Whiskey business still feeling effects of 2018 Trump tariffs
item.description: "Every single time there's retaliatory tariffs, it's bourbon that gets hit," one business owner says.
item.dc:creator: Connell McShane, NewsNation

item.title: Karl Rove: Trump claims of DOGE 'fraud' discoveries will backfire
item.description: Republican strategist Karl Rove agreed with James Carville, who had urged Democrats to make a "strategic political retreat," advising them "to play dead.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, NewsNation

item.title: OPM inspector general investigating DOGE emails, access to agency data
item.description: The inspector general for the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) told lawmakers he is investigating the use of a new data system at the agency that has been part of a broader effort by Elon Musk and his allies to email federal workers.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Harvard freezes hiring amid financial 'uncertainties' driven by Trump policies
item.description: Harvard University says it is freezing hiring for faculty and staff amid financial “uncertainties” driven by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: D.C.'s Black Lives Matter mural will be erased. Look back at the iconic street painting
item.description: Over the past five years, Washington, D.C.'s iconic Black Lives Matter street painting has served as a powerful symbol of activism and a gathering place for joy and resistance.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Renewed debate sparks discussion on 'My Safe Florida Home' program
item.description: Governor Ron DeSantis wants more Floridians to benefit from the "My Safe Florida Home" program, which provides storm-hardening home improvements for homeowners.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Social Security offices in NY set to close amid DOGE budget cuts
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Several Social Security offices in New York will be closing as the Department of Government Efficiency continues to cut federal spending. The closures come after the Social Security Administration said it found over $800 million in savings for the 2025 fiscal year.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Aces could join Las Vegas teams with their own specialty license plates
item.description: Las Vegas Aces fans might get a chance to show their support as the state considers issuing a specialty license plate.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Las Vegas homeless camps just move when outreach comes, but proposal might change that
item.description: When homeless camps get out of control, local governments send teams to clean up. That can be a disaster, costing people what few possessions they have. But trash and unsanitary conditions are becoming a bigger priority as residents go to elected officials demanding action.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida House speaker responds to Democrats call to investigate insurance companies
item.description: Democrats at the Florida State House are calling on Gov. Ron DeSantis and the House Speaker to further investigate Florida's insurance companies.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US paused Ukraine intelligence support: Trump officials
item.description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe and national security adviser Michael Waltz both said the U.S. had paused intelligence sharing in addition to military aid.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump passes Clinton, gives longest modern address to joint session of Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump gave the longest address to a joint session of Congress in modern history on Tuesday night, surpassing former President Bill Clinton’s previous mark.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5
